text:
REGIME — Soft CPI sparked a broad risk-on bid; defensives and tech diverged sharply.

INDICES — VIX 19.5, falling. IJR +0.71%, QQQ +0.68%, SPY +0.58%, DIA +0.57%, IWM +0.57%. Small caps outperformed large caps on the day. VIXY fell -3.64%, the most notable cross-asset move, confirming fear unwind.

SECTORS — XLY +1.73%, XLI +1.03%, XLP +0.81%, XLU +0.79%, XLRE +0.68%, XLC +0.40%, XLK +0.27%, XLF +0.25%, XLB -0.15%, XLV -0.23%, XLE -1.24%. Top industries: WGMI +2.10%, USO +1.91%. Bottom industries: CIBR -3.93%, WCLD -5.65%.

THE 7s — Fab 7 +2.64%, Semi 7 +2.41%, Buildout 7 +2.07%, Industrial 7 +1.73%, New Staples 7 +1.68%, Old Staples 7 +0.97%, Bank 7 +0.88%, Freight 7 +0.77%, Housing 7 +0.59%, Power 7 +0.16%, Insurance 7 +0.09%, Health 7 -0.01%, New Metals 7 -0.42%, Energy 7 -0.59%, Defense 7 -0.60%, Old Metals 7 -0.85%, Cloud 7 -0.90%, Medtech 7 -1.11%, Toll Road 7 -1.47%, Software 7 -5.48%. Strongest ticker in Fab 7 not individually disclosed; weakest in Software 7 was NET -8.62%.

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC Apr 29, 2026; CME holds at 100% hold, 0% cut/hike. Fed tally: 6 hawks, 5 neutral, 4 doves. USD Liquidity Stress 48/100 (Normal); most-stressed component is Reserve Scarcity at 80th percentile. TGA dropped -$99.3B; ON-RRP sits at a negligible $0.4B.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 62.9, Greed. Top movers: NET -8.62% (Software 7), NOW -7.86% (Software 7), PLTR -7.30% (Software 7). 20d momentum: best sector XLF +3.94%, worst Software 7 -14.12%; best basket Semi 7 +11.89%, worst Software 7 -14.12%. Soft CPI MoM 0.27% and Core CPI MoM 0.22% drove the broad risk-on lift while Energy lagged on stalled Middle East ceasefire news.

WATCH — NET today -8.62%, vol 3.2x average: gap down on earnings or guidance deserves follow-through assessment. NOW -7.86%, vol 2.6x: watch if software selling broadens into mega-cap tech tomorrow. XLE -1.24% while USO gained +1.91%: unusual divergence between oil price and equity energy warrants monitoring for catch-up or further breakdown.
